mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or stays at very low levels, the discount rate on future earnings declines and equity duration rises, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the sign reverses when CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates labor-market stress and higher recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are cut and risk premia widen.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not materially below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales RSAFS are rising while housing starts HOUST are also rising, broad domestic demand is accelerating across consumption and construction, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger revenue growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST expansion does not outperform either series alone and does not raise 60-day SPX returns relative to baseline over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO after a prior contraction signals improving industrial supply conditions and tighter cyclical earnings momentum, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price a near-term growth rebound.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances after the trigger, or if the effect disappears once revision-risk months are excluded.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS level above neutral raises financing costs and compresses valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative when CPIAUCSL is also elevated. The inflation condition prevents false positives from low-rate environments.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes with elevated CPIAUCSL do not show worse 20-day SPX returns than low-inflation high-rate episodes, or if the conditional hit rate is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 indicates weakening final demand and lower expected corporate revenues, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as analysts revise growth forecasts downward.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP declines are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the sign is positive after controlling for recent market drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth is positive and UNRATE is falling at the same time, labor-market strength supports disposable income and consumer spending, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition fails to produce positive 20-day returns in a majority of observations, or if either variable alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: If HOUST is rising while RSAFS is flat or declining, housing-sensitive demand is improving faster than general consumption, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through cyclical sector leadership and sentiment spillovers.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases for 2 months while RSAFS does not improve over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the housing-led relative-strength condition does not outperform baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when RSAFS is included as a separate additive predictor.
